feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Joint-Pain Supplement Tied to Faster Alzheimer's Progression
entry.summary: Researchers say findings on glucosamine are preliminary and show association, not causation

entry.title: The omega-3 supplement you take may not be helping your brain
entry.summary: Taking an omega-3 fatty acid supplement did not improve memory, cognitive function or brain cell loss for many people, a surprising new study finds.

entry.title: Scientists Link 8 Common Food Additives to Heart Disease Risk
entry.summary: Certain food preservatives might silently be raising our blood pressure and heart disease risk, recent research has found.

entry.title: Rare Lyme disease-causing strain spreads to new state: What to know
entry.summary: A rare pathogen known to cause Lyme disease has been detected in a new state, far from where it has typically been found.

entry.title: On TikTok, sunscreen misinformation goes viral
entry.summary: Sunscreen videos on TikTok often go viral, but the most popular may spread misinformation, a PLOS Digital Health study found. University of Alberta study.

entry.title: Mom Thought She Had a Boil. Then She Vomited 'Black Tar', Ended Up in a Coma from Flesh-Eating Disease
entry.summary: Caroline Fonjock needed emergency surgery after she developed an aggressive case of the flesh-eating disease that left her leg looking 'like roadkill'

entry.title: Shingles vaccine(s) and dementia: the only medical intervention to work faster than Tums
entry.summary: Do you believe in magic?

entry.title: Cancer patients are choosing ivermectin over chemo, and doctors are concerned
entry.summary: Ivermectin, the anti-parasitic medication most commonly used as a dewormer in animals, is back in the limelight. This time it has nothing to do with COVID-19.

entry.title: Your pelvic floor weakens as you get older. Here’s how to keep it strong for life.
entry.summary: Weakness in this part of the body becomes increasingly common with age, causing urinary incontinence and other symptoms. But it’s also treatable.

entry.title: 42 babies die in Australia and cases of devastating STI spike in US — doc warns he’s ‘never been more concerned’
entry.summary: Left untreated, it can lead to miscarriage, premature birth, skeletal abnormalities, neurological problems, developmental delays, stillbirth or infant death shortly after birth.

entry.title: Hunter-gatherers in Siberia died of a plague outbreak 5,500 years ago
entry.summary: We can't blame the Neolithic Transition for the plague anymore.

entry.title: The Number-1 Sign of Colon Cancer Doctors Say You Shouldn’t Ignore
entry.summary: Gastroenterologists reveal the number-one sign of colon cancer. Plus, other symptoms to watch for and why the illness can be difficult to diagnose.

entry.title: The head of the family is 17. Money is tight. The roof leaks. How did this happen?
entry.summary: Three brothers say their mother and father died after losing access to their HIV medications. Now the boys are figuring out how to navigate life.

entry.title: South African scientists make breakthrough in decoding cancer’s most effective survival strategy
entry.summary: The goal is to develop treatments that can strip away the sugar shield cancer uses to hide from the immune system.

entry.title: AI helped diagnose 18 children whose rare diseases had stumped doctors
entry.summary: Researchers from Boston Children’s Hospital identified 18 diagnoses for children with rare diseases using OpenAI’s o3 model.

entry.title: 7 unexpected takeaways from the newest research on cannabis and brain effects
entry.summary: Whether it’s used in adolescence, midlife or older age may make a big difference.

entry.title: Researchers found that people who often share meals with others tend to report greater wellbeing, a link about as strong as income or having a job
entry.summary: Most days I leave my place around 1pm and go to lunch, usually alone. It is the seam in my working day, the bit between the morning at my desk and the afternoon spent moving between coffee shops. For a long time I thought of it as unremarkable. Fuel, a break,…

entry.title: Quartz countertops are driving a public health crisis in the US – 2 occupational health experts explain the surge of lung transplants and lawsuits
entry.summary: Workers are facing a preventable and incurable lung disease from a material being used to renovate kitchens in millions of American homes.

entry.title: 4 Things a Cardiologist Never Does Before 9 a.m. for His Own Heart Health
entry.summary: Heart health can be impacted by breakfast food and drink. Cardiologist shares the morning habits he avoids to prevent heart disease and his favorite breakfast.

entry.title: Study ranks these 10 states as the worst for heart disease deaths
entry.summary: Heart disease is the leading cause of death in the U.S, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention says.

entry.title: Zero sugar, more problems? Study reveals surprising gut health effects
entry.summary: A study presented at ENDO 2026 suggests that completely eliminating sugar from a low-fat diet may harm gut health and increase inflammation.

entry.title: What The Presence — Or Absence — Of Toe Hair Can Tell You About Your Health
entry.summary: Whether you have a little or a lot, there are a few things you should know.

entry.title: The 8 Best Beans for Your Health, Ranked by Dietitians
entry.summary: All beans are packed with fiber, protein, and key minerals and antioxidants, but some are more nutritious than others. Dietitians compare popular beans like pinto beans, chickpeas, and black beans based on their nutritional benefits.

entry.title: Statins Have Been Misunderstood for Years. Here’s What the Science Actually Says
entry.summary: Despite decades of use—and data supporting their efficacy and safety—cholesterol-lowering meds are being targeted by influencers and pseudoscience experts.

entry.title: The Sperm-Maxxing Bros Are Actually Onto Something
entry.summary: Wellness influencers have stumbled onto a huge issue when it comes male fertility, though not every solution they're pitching is good advice.

entry.title: Doctor Exposes Shocking Studies On Women’s Health, And People Are Absolutely Furious
entry.summary: Women’s health concerns are dismissed far too often, but one doctor recently shared an example that still managed to shock viewers. After calling out two endometriosis studies on TikTok, she showed just how low the bar can get. Instead of helping women with t…

entry.title: Rheumatologist Shares 4 Signs of Autoimmune Disease That Are ‘Easy To Miss’
entry.summary: A doctor has revealed four symptoms, when combined, could point to a rare autoimmune disease many people don’t realize they have.

entry.title: An Irresponsible Sister Demanded to Reconnect With Her Estranged Daughter. Why Her Family Said Absolutely Not.
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: A Countertop Material Found in Countless Kitchens Is at the Center of a Public Health Debate
entry.summary: This May, California's workplace safety board voted to advance to the next steps for a proposed ban on engineered stone. Made of stone particles held together with an adhesive, the fabrication and installation of engineered stone like quartz has been linked t…

entry.title: Mushrooms can provide up to 1200 IU Vitamin D per 100 grams if they are cooked like this
entry.summary: Mushrooms are said to be nutrient-rich superfoods packed with surprising health benefits. They are low in calories and fat, and are a great source of B vitamins, selenium, potassium, and powerful antioxidants that support immunity and fight inflammation. Did …

entry.title: 'I buried my parents one day after the other'
entry.summary: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.

entry.title: Humboldt Public Health Officials Confirm Local Whooping Cough Case, Warn of Potential Exposures in Arcata, McKinleyville and Fortuna
entry.summary: Most people think of pertussis, commonly known as whooping cough, as a disease from the past. Unfortunately, it is still a problem and tends to flare up every three to seven years in communities. Right now, staff from the Public Health Branch of the Departmen…

entry.title: A Bone Health Expert Shares Their Most Important Tip
entry.summary: Maintaining strong bones as you age requires more than your daily dose of calcium.

entry.title: Ozempic and Wegovy linked to drop in violent behavior, study finds
entry.summary: The study found patients taking Ozempic and Wegovy were noticeably less impulsive and less prone to violence.

entry.title: Study Reveals Optimal Number of Daily Steps to Offset Sitting Down
entry.summary: You've probably heard that adults should aim for 10,000 daily steps.

entry.title: Cervical cancer deaths fall to zero in young women given vaccine
entry.summary: A new study finds that hundreds of lives have been saved since school-age girls were offered the HPV jab in 2008.

entry.title: Hunter-gatherer cemeteries reveal an ancient plague’s earliest known victims
entry.summary: Hunter-gatherer communities, especially young children, were hit hard by an early strain of plague 5,500 years ago.

entry.title: Mapping the neuronal building blocks of human language with language models
entry.summary: Wide-scale recordings reveal neurons in the human brain that encode fundamental components of language such as the grammatical relationships between words, their parts of speech and the higher-order syntactic structure of phrases and …

entry.title: ‘Sad Nipple Syndrome’ Causes Distress Among Women
entry.summary: One woman’s viral video sparked debate over a phenomenon called “sad nipples.”

entry.title: A mosaic of whole-body representations on the human precentral gyrus
entry.summary: A comprehensive map of the human motor cortex at single-neuron resolution is described.

entry.title: Lethal plague outbreaks in Lake Baikal hunter-gatherers 5,500 years ago
entry.summary: Analyses of ancient DNA from hunter-gatherers near Lake Baikal in southeast Siberia around 5,500 years ago indicate that highly virulent Yersinia pestis emerged earlier than previously estimated, far from the next known cases of infection in La…

entry.title: Cortical development dynamics across autism spectrum disorder mouse models
entry.summary: Using single-nucleus multi-omic sequencing, diverse autism spectrum disorder-linked gene mutations converge on transient, stage-specific disruptions in early brain development, and highlight sex-specific gene expression alterations.

entry.title: Trial Drug Could Clear Toxic Alzheimer’s Proteins and Restore Memory
entry.summary: Researchers uncovered an unexpected way to restore the brain’s cleanup network, hinting at a fresh strategy against dementia.

entry.title: New Research Pinpoints Hidden Brain Trigger That Causes Autism, Changing How We Understand The Condition
entry.summary: “The autism diagnosis has dramatically increased. It's alarming,” a netizen said.

entry.title: A clearer picture of drinking and disease
entry.summary: New study attempts to reconcile conflicting findings on benefits vs. risks.

entry.title: Binge-eating mystery affecting millions of women may finally be solved: study
entry.summary: Researchers identify a medical connection tied to binge eating in women, shedding light on cravings and eating pattern behaviors.

entry.title: How the brain builds sentences, neuron by neuron
entry.summary: Neural maps reveal the specialized cells that produce speech.

entry.title: Immune system may attack nervous system in some Long COVID patients
entry.summary: Researchers linked antibodies that attack the body’s nervous system to some neurological symptoms of Long COVID.

entry.title: Life-threatening meat allergy caused by tick bites is on the rise. Here’s what you should know
entry.summary: Health officials are trying to raise awareness about a life-threatening allergy to meat triggered by tick bites.

entry.title: This Deadly and Entirely Preventable Disease Is On the Rise. Here's How You Can Stay Safe
entry.summary: According to CDC data, tetanus cases are increasing in the United States. Doctors explain what's going on and how you can protect yourself.
